titleStmt.title: SII 19.46: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 46. (A.R. No. 439 of 1908.) TIRUVIḺIMILALAI, NANNILAM TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT. ON A PILLAR IN THE SUNDARAKUCHĀMBĀḶ SHRINE IN THE VIḶINĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE.

summary.p: This records the gift of a silver dish weighing 240 kaḻañju to hold the daily śrībali offerings to God Paramēśvara-Bhaṭāra of Tiruviḻimiḻalai by Uḍaiyār Tāntōṉri. Tanichchēvagar, by which is perhaps meant the king himself as the ‘devoted servant’ of God Tāntōṉṟi. Tiruviḻimiḻalai is said to have been a muṟṟūṭṭu-dēvadāṉa of (god) Veṇṇāṭṭu-Perumāṉār. This is engraved immediately below No. 438 of 1908 mentioning one Arikulakēsari-Viḻupparaiyaṉ. This might be assigned to Ariñjaya, instead of to Vijayālaya as is surmised in M.E.R. for 1909. Part II, para 35.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . kopparakesaripa
nmarkku yāṇṭu 2 Āvatiṉ E
tirāmāṇṭu meṣanāyaṟṟu ve
ṇṇāṭṭup perumāṉār muṟṟū
ṭṭut tevatāṉan tirumaḻalai
t tiruviḻimiḻalai Uṭaiya parame
śvarabhaṭṭārarkku śrīpali koṇ
ṭeḻun=taruḷa
śrīpalittāla
māka Uṭaiyār
tāṉtoṉṟi tta
(ta)ṉiccevaka
r Iṭṭa śrīpali
ttālam veḷ
ḷḷi niṟai Iru
nūṟṟu nāṟpa
tiṉ kaḻaiñcu
Itu panmāhe
śvararakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 19.46 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
